plan.summary: Create EventHero.js with a scoped ResizeObserver that caps .top-gradient max-height to the nearest <main> element, targeting only .top-gradient elements inside .event-hero wrappers. No CSS file is needed because EventHero already inherits the .top-gradient styles (including the 150vh height) from Hero.css via the shared 'hero' class on its <section> element.

existingState.topGradientElement: Already exists in both EventHero.php (line 80) and EventHero.tsx (line 213) as
existingState.cssSource: Inherited from blocks/Hero/Hero.css via the shared 'hero' class. Hero.css defines .top-gradient with height: 150vh, position: absolute, gradient backgrounds. No EventHero-specific CSS file exists or is needed.
existingState.jsSource: No EventHero.js exists. Hero.js currently targets all .top-gradient elements globally, but the parallel Hero edit (orchestrator-state for hero block) plans to scope Hero.js to only .hero wrappers (not .event-hero). Therefore EventHero needs its own scoped JS.
existingState.blockWrapperClass: event-hero (from theme_block_props call in PHP line 77: 'event-hero hero relative overflow-visible')
existingState.webpackBundling: webpack.config.js line 33 uses glob pattern './blocks/**/!(*index).js' which will auto-bundle EventHero.js into screen.js -- no block.json viewScript registration needed

fileChanges.description: Create frontend JS with ResizeObserver to cap .top-gradient max-height to <main> element height, scoped to .event-hero wrappers only
fileChanges.details: Follow the Hero.js pattern but scope querySelectorAll to '.event-hero .top-gradient' instead of '.top-gradient'. Use DOMContentLoaded listener, find nearest <main> via closest() or fallback to document.querySelector('main'), create ResizeObserver on <main> that sets gradient.style.maxHeight = main.offsetHeight + 'px'. This ensures the gradient never extends beyond the main content area.

plan.blockJsonChanges: None required. The JS file is auto-bundled via webpack glob pattern, not via block.json viewScript.
plan.phpChanges: None. The .top-gradient element and the 'hero' class (which provides CSS inheritance) already exist.
plan.tsxChanges: None. The .top-gradient element already exists in the editor component.

designDecisions.reason: EventHero's <section> already has the 'hero' class which inherits all .top-gradient styles from Hero.css, including the 150vh height. Creating a duplicate CSS file would violate DRY.

The parallel Hero block edit is scoping Hero.js to only target .hero-specific wrappers. Each hero variant (Hero, PostHero, EventHero) needs its own scoped JS to avoid cross-block interference. Using '.event-hero .top-gradient' ensures only EventHero instances are targeted.

designDecisions.decision: Standalone JS file rather than inline PHP script
designDecisions.reason: Follows the established Hero.js pattern. Avoids PHP function validator false-positives on JavaScript identifiers. Webpack auto-bundles via glob pattern.

plan.qaNotes: Verify: (1) EventHero.js is bundled into screen.js after build, (2) ResizeObserver sets max-height on .top-gradient inside .event-hero, (3) .top-gradient height remains 150vh from inherited Hero.css, (4) gradient does not overflow past <main> boundary.
